system.description:
The illusion requires the scale, claw, fur, or some other part of any type of dragon, which acts as a focus for the casting. The illusion of a shadowy dragon's head appears floating above the illusionist. The head grants the illusionist a +2 to AC. Also, when anyone attacks the illusionist, the dragon's head bites at the attacker, as long as the attacker is within range. If the victim fails an intelligence save they suffer 3d6 damage. The head remains for 1 round per level of the caster, or until it bites.

The material component for this spell is the dust of a dragon.
